question: What do I need to do to be eligible for the certificate if I’m joining late?
answer_agent:
If you’re joining late, you can still be eligible for the certificate as long as you **finish the course with the live cohort** and **pass the Capstone project**.

A few important notes:
- **Homework is not mandatory** for the certificate.
- You **cannot get a certificate in self-paced mode**.
- The reason is that certificate eligibility depends on being able to **peer-review 3 capstones**, which is only possible while the course is running.

So, to be eligible: **join the live cohort in time to complete and submit the Capstone, and participate in the required peer review.**

question: Where do I find the live stream for Office Hours or workshop sessions if the Zoom link isn’t public?
answer_agent:
The Zoom link is only shared with instructors/presenters/TAs, not publicly with students.

To watch live:
- Check the **announcements channel on Telegram and Slack** before it starts — the video URL is posted there.
- You can also watch on the **DataTalksClub YouTube channel**.
- During the live session, submit questions via **Slido** (the link is pinned in chat when live).

Don’t rely on chat for questions, since they may get missed.

question: Where do I begin if I want to follow this course properly, and which pages or repo should I check first?
answer_agent:
To follow the course properly, start here first:

1. **LLM Zoomcamp docs**  
   https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/

2. **General Zoomcamp logistics docs**  
   https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/

3. **Main GitHub repository**  
   https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp

The **GitHub repo is the main entry point** and is usually the best place to check first because it stays updated more reliably than the YouTube playlist.

A typical workflow is:
- Watch the lesson videos
- Work through the notebooks/code in the repo
- Read the homework instructions on GitHub
- Submit your answers on the course platform before the deadline

If you want, I can also help you figure out the exact order to study week by week.

question: I can’t find my name on the leaderboard — how do I locate my own entry?
answer_agent:
You’re probably on the leaderboard under a random display name. To find your own entry:

1. Open the leaderboard.
2. Click **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”**.

If you want to see what name you’re listed under, go to **Edit Course Profile**:
- **First field** = your public display name / nickname on the leaderboard
- **Second field** = your official name for the certificate

If you want, I can also explain how to change your display name.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!
tool_calls: [{'name': 'search', 'arguments': '{"query":"leaderboard find my name locate my own entry"}'}]

question: Why do I show up under some random name instead of my real one on the leaderboard?
answer_agent:
You’re seeing a random name because the leaderboard uses a separate **display name** that is assigned automatically when you create your account. It’s anonymous by default, so it may look like something like “Lucid Elbakyan.”

If you want to change it:

1. Go to **Edit Course Profile**
2. Update the **first field** — this is your public nickname/display name shown on the leaderboard
3. Update the **second field** only if you want to change the name printed on your certificate

So your real name does **not** appear on the leaderboard unless you set it there yourself.

question: Can I do this course on my own schedule and still get the certificate, or is it only for the live cohort?
answer_agent:
No — according to the FAQ, you can follow the course in self-paced mode, but you **won’t get a certificate** that way.

You only get a certificate if you finish the course with a **live cohort**, because the certificate requires **peer-reviewing capstone projects**, and that only happens while the course is running.

No, you can only get a certificate if you finish the course with a "live" cohort.

We don't award certificates for the self-paced mode. The reason is you need to peer-review 3 capstone(s) after submitting your project.

You can only peer-review projects at the time the course is running; after the form is closed and the peer-review list is compiled.

No. You can only peer-review the required projects while the course is still running. After the submission form is closed and the peer-review list is compiled, reviews can’t be done anymore.

If your goal is a certificate, you need to finish with a live cohort and complete the required peer reviews before the course ends.
answer_orig:
No, you can only get a certificate if you finish the course with a "live" cohort.

We don't award certificates for the self-paced mode. The reason is you need to peer-review 3 capstone(s) after submitting your project.

You can only peer-review projects at the time the course is running; after the form is closed and the peer-review list is compiled.

question: If homework isn't mandatory, why should I bother doing it at all?
answer_agent:
Homework isn’t mandatory for the certificate, but it still has value:

- It helps you practice the material and reinforce what you learned.
- Homework points count toward your leaderboard rank.
- It gives you a chance to apply the lesson workflow on a slightly different problem.

So if you want the best learning experience and a better leaderboard position, it’s worth doing.

question: Why does the homework keep changing while I’m working on it?
answer_agent:
If the homework title contains **`[DRAFT]`**, it means the homework isn’t ready yet, so the content can still change while you’re working on it.

It’s only considered ready when **both** are true:
- the homework form is open on the course management platform
- the homework title does **not** contain **`[DRAFT]`**

Until then, it’s best to treat it as work-in-progress, since the final version may be different.
answer_orig:
If the homework title contains `[DRAFT]`, it means the homework is not ready yet.

The homework is ready only when both are true:

- The homework form is open on the course management platform.
- The homework title does not contain `[DRAFT]`.

Until then, the content can still change. Working on the material or homework in advance is at your own risk, because the final version can be different.
